name: Engineered E. coli colanic-acid variants
summary: Engineered E. coli colanic-acid variants are normalized for C. elegans lifespan screens of live microbial genetic composition.
mechanism_summary: Uses engineered bacterial strains and bacterial polysaccharide biology to probe microbiome-derived effects on host longevity in worms.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Engineered E. coli genetic variants extended C. elegans lifespan; Engineered E. coli genetic-composition C. elegans longevity screen.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
